question_title: Will the interest in “old dominion shooting” change between 2026-03-19 and 2026-03-27 according to Google Trends?
summary: The Old Dominion University shooting occurred March 12, 2026, with Google Trends interest peaking at 100 on March 13 and rapidly decaying to 4 by March 18. By the start date (March 19), interest will be at or near baseline (0-4 range). At these low absolute values, the ±3 threshold creates a wide "Doesn't change" band that absorbs most noise. "Decreases" is only possible if March 19 is still slightly elevated (4-5) and March 27 falls to 0, requiring specific value combinations. "Increases" requires a new catalyst; congressional hearings on homeland threats and the gun seller's court proceedings could generate modest interest but are unlikely to push this specific search term above the threshold. The empirical base rate from the series supports 76% no-change. Monte Carlo simulation yields ~82% DC, adjusted to 70% to account for residual elevation risk and measurement uncertainty.

factors.description: Interest has decayed to near-baseline (4 on Mar 18, down from peak 100 on Mar 13). Post-spike decay is nearly complete - the 'returned to baseline' regime strongly favors Doesn't Change.

At low absolute values (0-5), the ±3 threshold covers most of the possible range. If Mar 19 = 3, Decrease is mathematically impossible. If Mar 19 = 0-2, Decrease is impossible and Increase needs value ≥4-6.
Empirical base rates from full series: 76.2% no change, 14.3% decrease, 9.5% increase (n=21, ±3 threshold).
If Mar 19 is still slightly elevated (4-5) and decays to 0 by Mar 27, this crosses the -3 threshold. Mar 18 was 4, so Mar 19 being 4-5 is plausible (~35% chance).

Congressional hearings on homeland threats starting Mar 19 will mention ODU shooting. Gun seller Chapman's court proceedings ongoing. Secondary stories unlikely to produce >3 point increase in specific search term.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty of ±1-2 points at low values could shift outcome across ±3 threshold.

reasoning:
**Forecast: "Old Dominion Shooting" Google Trends Change (Mar 19-27)**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| **Doesn't change** | **70%** |
| Decreases | 17% |
| Increases | 13% |

**Key reasoning:**

- The shooting occurred March 12, peaked at 100 on March 13, and has decayed to 4 by March 18 — nearly back to baseline
- At these low values (0-5), the ±3 threshold creates a wide "Doesn't change" zone that absorbs most noise
- **Decreases** is plausible only if March 19 is still slightly elevated (4-5) and March 27 drops to 0 — a specific but realistic scenario
- **Increases** requires a new catalyst; congressional hearings on homeland threats (starting Mar 19) and the gun seller's court proceedings provide modest catalyst risk, but are secondary stories unlikely to drive the specific term above threshold
- Empirical base rate from the series: 76.2% no change, supporting the dominant outcome
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “old dominion shooting” change between 2026-03-19 and 2026-03-27 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42718)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=old%20dominion%20shooting&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=old%20dominion%20shooting&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27) for 2026-03-27 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-19. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "old dominion shooting",
  "date": "2026-02-25 2026-03-27",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
